# services.scx

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.scx`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.scx.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable SCX service, a daemon to run schedulers from userspace. ::: {.note} This service requires a kernel with the Sched-ext feature. Generally, kernel version 6.12 and later are supported. ::: |
| `services.scx.extraArgs` | `list of string` | Parameters passed to the chosen scheduler at runtime. ::: {.note} Run `chosen-scx-scheduler --help` to see the available options. Generally, each scheduler has its own set of options, and they are incompatible with each other. ::: |
| `services.scx.package` | `package` | `scx` package to use. `scx.full`, which includes all schedulers, is the default. You may choose a minimal package, such as `pkgs.scx.rustscheds`. ::: {.note} Overriding this does not change the default scheduler; you should set `services.scx.scheduler` for it. ::: |
| `services.scx.scheduler` | `one of "scx_central", "scx_flatcg", "scx_nest", "scx_pair", "scx_prev", "scx_qmap", "scx_simple", "scx_userland", "scx_beerland", "scx_bpfland", "scx_cake", "scx_chaos", "scx_cosmos", "scx_flash", "scx_flow", "scx_lavd", "scx_layered", "scx_mitosis", "scx_p2dq", "scx_pandemonium", "scx_rlfifo", "scx_rustland", "scx_rusty", "scx_tickless"` | Which scheduler to use. See [SCX documentation](https://github.com/sched-ext/scx/tree/main/scheds) for details on each scheduler and guidance on selecting the most suitable one. |
